title: ACDP Registry Receipt (0.2.0)
description: A registry-signed attestation binding the registry-assigned identity fields of one context, the producer's content_hash, and the fingerprint of the producer key resolved at publish time, to the registry's DID. Specified by RFC-ACDP-0010 (acdp/0.2.0); the wire shape is the one reserved by RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.7 since v0.1.0, which is why this schema lives in the v0.1.0 namespace (additive minor version — see VERSIONING.md). The receipt preimage is the JCS canonicalization of this object minus 'signature'; the registry signs the ASCII bytes of 'sha256:<hex(SHA-256(preimage))>' exactly as RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.8.

registry_did.description: The minting registry's DID. MUST be did:web:<authority> where <authority> equals the serving authority (RFC-ACDP-0010 §4, §8 step 2). did:web-only even in 0.2.0.

origin_registry.description: Bare DNS hostname; MUST equal the ctx_id authority and the registry_did method-specific identifier (RFC-ACDP-0010 §4).

created_at.description: Registry-assigned creation timestamp. Unlike the accept-any-precision rfc3339_timestamp type, the receipt pins the canonical millisecond form exactly (RFC-ACDP-0010 §8 step 6); it MUST be byte-identical to body.created_at.

content_hash.description: The body's ProducerContent hash as verified by the registry at publish time. Consumers compare against their independently recomputed value, never the echoed body field (RFC-ACDP-0010 §8 step 4).

key_fingerprint.description: sha256:<lowercase hex SHA-256> over the raw public-key bytes of the producer key resolved at publish time: the 32-byte raw Ed25519 key or the 33-byte SEC1-compressed P-256 point (RFC-ACDP-0010 §6). Test vectors: fp-001-key-fingerprint-vectors.json.

signature.description: The registry's signature over the receipt hash (RFC-ACDP-0010 §5). signature.key_id MUST be a DID URL under registry_did; retired receipt keys remain resolvable via the registry DID document's verificationMethod per RFC-ACDP-0010 §9.

$comment: Closed schema (RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3.1 openness map): every receipt field is signed, so an unknown member would change the preimage; receipt extensions (e.g. the reserved log_inclusion member, RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.11) require an explicit schema bump.
